feed.title: Silesh.com
feed.subtitle: My personal blog. Check out the latest post or select via Categories

entry.title: E-COM 2- Setting up MongoDB to use with Java and Spring

link.title: E-COM 2- Setting up MongoDB to use with Java and Spring

entry.summary: This post will go over how to setup MongoDB (atlas) and move the storage of our product list over rather than keep it in memory. We will also see how to make use of spring repository to make crud operations and object mapping from DB much easier. Getting started with MongoDB Create an account on mongodb.com. Then create a new cluster on MongoDB Atlas. We can then click on connect to create a d...

entry.title: E-COM 1- Building a REST server and APIs with Java and Spring

link.title: E-COM 1- Building a REST server and APIs with Java and Spring

entry.summary: This post will serve as the starting point for a lot of big things to come. The plan is to incrementally develop a project (an e-commerece application) and experiment with and implement various industry relevant features such as load balancing, caching, synchronization etc. To do this we need a starting point. In this post I will cover the basics of getting started with a simple REST server wit...

entry.title: What is a noSQL DB, and why you should use one.

link.title: What is a noSQL DB, and why you should use one.

entry.summary: Most people know what’s a relational Database, you create tables, insert data, perform joins and run queries with SQL. But this is not your only option if you need a database. Something that’s been gaining a lot of traction lately is the use of NoSQL databases. I’ve unfortunately never had to work with a noSQL database and I am remedying that with this post, by understanding exactly what these ...

entry.title: N Queens problem
entry.summary: N queens is a basic backtracking problem that drives home some very important concepts. The problem statement is as follows: Place n queens on an n x n chessboard such that no two queens attack each other. For example, for n = 4 and n=8: Can be satisfied with the following arrangements: A solution for n=4 A solution for n=8 Essentially the problem is one that involves searching for valid a...

entry.title: LRU Cache implementation
entry.summary: LRU or least recently used cache is a very frequently used cache implementation. Here I’ll list out its properties and then implement it in a very simple manner. An LRU cache is a type of fixed size data structure that keeps the most recently used item at the front. When the cache is full and a new item needs to be added, the oldest item (least recently used) is removed and the new one is adde...
